content.claim: Early selective tracks in Hungary produce higher value-added in reading and mathematical literacy and tertiary-education continuation for higher-status students, while weaker students in general tracks experience lower gains, increasing inequality of opportunity.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Panel data design does not rule out unobserved confounding by family motivation or prior achievement
Value-added interpretation depends on statistical model specification
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
